name: importlib.reload(phillypug)
description:
We're excited to announce that the Philadelphia Python Users Group (PhillyPUG) is back! After a hiatus, we're rebooting and kicking off with our first meetup May 28th with a couple talks and some pizza.

​🗓️ **Agenda**

* [6:00-6:45] Doors Open
* ​​Grab some food/drink
* ​​Meet the attendees
* ​​[6:45-7:00] Welcome & Intro
* ​​[7:00-7:20] (Talk) Docker for Python Users
* [​​7:20-7:40] (Talk) Enum Deep Cuts: Lessons from Enums in ty
* ​​[7:40-8:30] Wind Down/Hang Out
* ​​Grab food, hang out, meet local devs.
* ​​Sign up to become a speaker!

🗣️ **Speakers**

**Aaron Brock** is currently Director of Gen A.I. Cloud Security at S&P Global. Aaron has extensive experience in Docker, K8s, Jenkins, Ansible, Terraform and more. He has worked at a wide breadth of companies across a variety of industries ranging from small gaming companies to large financial institutions, and has experience at every level of the software development lifecycle. [Website](https://automateaaron.com/), [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/automateaaron/)

**Justin Chapman** is CTO at Anuvi and a former engineer at Meta where he worked on the Instagram feed. He contributes occasionally to ty (Astral's Python type checker), and is currently working on [tryke](https://github.com/thejchap/tryke), a Python test runner. [GitHub](https://github.com/thejchap), [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/justinchapman1/)

**🎤 Call for speakers!**
Have an idea for a talk? Have something interesting to share or teach? We'd love to host you!

name: AI Policy and Governance Overview | Virtual
description:
​**A free, public workshop—no technical background needed**
​AI is moving fast. The companies building it are moving fast. Governments are trying to respond, but most people still don’t have a clear picture of what is actually happening or what good policy would even look like.
​This workshop is meant to give people that bigger picture.
​We’ll start with a quick, plain-English overview of what AI is as a technology, how today’s systems work at a high level, and why the last few years have felt so different from the AI progress that came before. From there, we’ll look at the new industry forming around frontier AI: the major labs, the incentives they face, the race to build more capable models, and why that race has raised serious questions about safety and governance.
​Then we’ll move into AI safety: what people mean by it, why it matters, and why this is not just a technical issue for researchers in labs. AI already creates real problems today, from scams and misinformation to bias, privacy concerns, cyber risks, and labor disruption. More advanced models could raise the stakes further, especially if they become better at persuasion, hacking, autonomous planning, scientific research, or acting on behalf of users in the real world.
​Together, we’ll explore questions like:

* ​**What is AI, really?** What makes today’s systems different from older software, and why are people treating this moment as a major technological shift?
* ​**What is AI safety?** What are researchers, policymakers, and companies worried about, and which concerns are immediate versus more forward-looking?
* ​**Current risks:** How is AI already affecting scams, misinformation, privacy, labor, education, and trust?
* ​**Near-future risks:** What could change if models become significantly more capable over the next year or two?
* ​**AI legislation:** What kinds of laws and regulations have been passed so far, what are they trying to do, and how has the debate around them gone?
* ​**Governance options:** What policy tools are being discussed to handle more serious risks from advanced AI systems?

​**What you’ll get:**

* ​A beginner-friendly overview of AI as a technology and industry
* ​A clear explanation of what AI safety means and why people disagree about it
* ​A grounded look at the risks AI poses today and the risks more advanced systems may pose soon
* ​A concrete overview of existing AI policy efforts and the debate around them
* ​A map of the main governance ideas being discussed, from transparency and evaluations to liability, licensing, compute governance, and international coordination

​This workshop is not about taking a simplistic “AI good” or “AI bad” position. The real world is messier than that.
​AI could bring enormous benefits, and it could also create serious problems if we build and deploy it carelessly. The goal of this workshop is to help you understand the policy conversation well enough to follow what is happening, evaluate proposals for yourself, and think clearly about what responsible governance might look like before the technology gets even more powerful.

name: Debug & Decaf
description:
Where:
**CHAR & STAVE CAFE**
**21 Rittenhouse Pl, Ardmore, PA 19003**

When your inbox finally shuts up, we gather for Debug & Decaf... a late-evening meetup for creators who know building in isolation sucks.

Hit a wall? Solved something worth sharing? Need feedback on a new feature?

**What Happens:**

* Founders share what they're stuck on...product decisions, technical challenges, go-to-market questions, whatever's keeping you up at night.
* The group actively debugs together (or seperate). Direct feedback, alternative approaches, solutions from different perspectives.
* Open laptops, real conversation, actual problem-solving.

**The Vibe:**
Less networking event in business attire, more late night office hours in sweats. Coffee's flowing. Feedback is direct and helpful. Nobody's selling anything.

**Dress code:** Sweatshirts and hoodies. Leave the button-downs at home.

name: Co-working with B+labs
description:
### **Event:** 🧬 Coffee & Code Co-Working: Building at B+labs

**Details:** Looking to break out of your usual routine and inject some fresh energy into your workday?
Join the Coffee & Code community for a special co-working session at **B+labs**! We are taking our laptops to the heart of Philadelphia's life sciences hub for a few hours of deep work, networking, and high-vibe collaboration.
B+labs hosts a premier life science incubator program for startups. Whether you are shipping production code, working on your next AI side project, or actively exploring the intersection of technology and biotech, this is the perfect space to get inspired and connect with brilliant minds in the space.

**What to Expect:**

* **10:30 AM – 11:00 AM:** Arrive, get settled, and say hello! ☕
* **11:00 AM – 3:30 PM:** Open Co-Working & Vibe Coding. Put your headphones on for deep work or collaborate with fellow builders. 💻
* **3:30 PM – 4:00 PM:** Wrap-up, casual networking, and sharing what we built/worked on today. 🚀

**Who Should Attend?** Developers, startup founders, life science innovators, or anyone in the tech ecosystem looking for a great environment to co-work alongside a supportive community.

**📍 Location:** B+labs 2929 Arch Street, Suite 200 (Located inside the Cira Centre)

**⏰ Time:** 10:30 AM – 4:00 PM
Space might be limited, so please RSVP to secure your spot. If you have any questions or have trouble finding the suite, just drop a message in the event chat.

Let’s build and connect! See you there. ✨

name: Composio Saturdays! Let your AI agents orchestrate your many apps in 3 hours!
description:
### 🚀 **Composio Sundays — Build, Share & Connect!**

Welcome to **Composio Sundays**, a relaxed weekly meetup for developers, builders, and AI enthusiasts who are exploring the future of AI agents and integrations with **Composio.dev** — the platform that empowers AI agents and LLMs to interact with real-world tools and workflows.
Whether you’re just getting started or shipping production-ready agents, this event is your space to learn, create, share, and collaborate with others in the ecosystem.
**What to Expect:**
✨ **Show & Tell** – Demo what you’ve built with Composio or tools that work alongside it.
🛠 **Hands-On Sessions** – Pair up to build integrations, troubleshoot toolchains, and explore new agent workflows.
💡 **Knowledge Exchange** – Tips, tricks, and best practices for integrating AI agents into real-world apps.
🤝 **Community Networking** – Meet fellow builders, share ideas, and find collaborators for your next project.
**Who Should Join:**
Developers, architects, product builders, and anyone curious about agentic AI, automation, and practical integrations with platforms like Composio.
**Why Attend:**
Composio makes connecting intelligent agents to external tools simpler and more reliable — and Composio Sundays is your weekly chance to level up with peers. Whether you're automating workflows or crafting production-ready systems, this meetup helps you turn ideas into action.

name: Diffusion Paper Reading Group
description:
**Explore the Future of Generative AI: Diffusion Models Reading Group**
Are you passionate about the cutting edge of machine learning and generative AI? Join our **Diffusion Models Reading Group**—a community of enthusiasts, researchers, and practitioners dedicated to exploring the fascinating world of diffusion-based generative models.

**📅 When:** Every Wednessday
**🕒 Time:** 800 PM - 900 PM EST
**📍 Where:** Online in our discord server

**About the Group:** Diffusion models have revolutionized the field of generative AI, enabling breakthroughs in image synthesis, data generation, and more. Our reading group is designed to dive deep into the latest research, discuss foundational concepts, and explore innovative applications of diffusion models.

**Where?**

* Online meeting link to the discord server is released on the day of the meetup via announcement!

**What to Expect:**

* **Weekly Discussions:** Engage in insightful conversations about recent papers, breakthroughs, and methodologies in diffusion modeling.
* **Collaborative Learning:** Share your ideas, ask questions, and collaborate on projects with fellow members.

**Who Should Join:**

* **Machine Learning Enthusiasts:** Whether you're a student, researcher, or industry professional, if you're interested in generative models, this group is for you.
* **Data Scientists & Engineers:** Enhance your skills and stay updated with the latest advancements in AI.

**Discussion Notes from previous paper readings:**

* Here is our Google Doc where we take notes (I added edit privileges for the OBNYC mailing list, and comment privileges for anyone who has the link; let me know if you need me to give you edit privileges separately):
* [https://docs.google.com/document/d/1-HtbGd1lGj68YEmgv8Ekpg0aI7TUmQVpAbRp498AVj8/edit?usp=sharing](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1-HtbGd1lGj68YEmgv8Ekpg0aI7TUmQVpAbRp498AVj8/edit?usp=sharing "https://docs.google.com/document/d/1-HtbGd1lGj68YEmgv8Ekpg0aI7TUmQVpAbRp498AVj8/edit?usp=sharing")

**See you there!**

Links:

Site: https://www.codephiladelphia.com/

Donate: https://hcb.hackclub.com/donations/start/code-and-coffee-philadelphia

Merch: https://nol-kky-shop.fourthwall.com/

name: Where is AI in 2026 and Where is it Going? | Virtual Workshop Series
description:
​**A free, public workshop with no technical background needed**
​For the last several years, AI systems have advanced at a pace that has stunned the public and even surprised leading experts. These systems are reshaping work, the economy, relationships, and even crime extremely quickly.
​This workshop will explore where AI may be in the next 1-5 years and what that means for daily life for all of us. We’ll tackle urgent and important questions like:

* ​**Jobs**: Could AI lead to mass unemployment or will it help create entirely new kinds of work?
* ​**Crime**: How might AI supercharge scams, hacking, and even crime?
* ​**Companions**: Will people form deep emotional bonds or even romantic relationships with AI companions?
* ​**Society**: How do top researchers think these trends will shape our future?

​**What you’ll get:**

* ​A fast-paced, beginner-friendly overview of today’s most powerful AI systems
* ​Live demos that reveal both the breakthroughs and the risk
* ​Interactive forecasting exercises where you’ll test your predictions about AI’s impact against the broader community
* ​Come ready to explore a future arriving faster than most people realize. A future that could transform not just how we work, but how we trust, connect, and even love.

*This is the first session in a six-part virtual series. Each workshop stands on its own and is free to attend. Future sessions: 6/4 AGI Transition, 6/9 Superintelligent AI, 6/11 Intro to AI Evals, 6/16 Data and Privacy in the Age of AI, 6/18 AI Policy and Governance Overview*

name: 13th Philly Hardware Meetup @ BFTP!
description:
Join us for the 13th Philadelphia Hardware Meetup! Come join us to hear from our guest speakers as well as share what’s on your desk. We always have time for an open mic.

***[Please click here to RSVP on Luma!](https://luma.com/fvfwpcjy)***

​​​This time, we'll be at **Ben Franklin Technology Partners** new office at 1600 Market Street. Check in with security to be directed to the event space.
​
*Hardware Meetups happen across 30+ cities monthly and help bring together different professionals to connect and meet each other.*
​​
**We have a great set of featured speakers for the evening!**

* **[Nicole Macnab](https://www.linkedin.com/in/nicole-macnab/)** is an RF Hardware and Embedded Systems Engineer at Deepwave. **[Deepwave](https://deepwave.ai/)** is developing both software and hardware to deliver real-time RF intelligence at the edge. Nicole assists in hardware development, as well as system-level integration and testing. She has Bachelor’s (C'2017) and Master’s (C'2023) degrees in Electrical Engineering from Rutgers University and Stevens Institute of Technology, respectively, with a minor in Mathematics.
* **[Arjun Nandakumar](https://www.linkedin.com/in/arjun-megharjun-nandakumar-493686a6/)** is the co-founder and CTO of **[Scout Robotics](https://scoutrobo.com/)**. (Bio coming soon!)

​**And pizza! Thanks to our host and sponsor, Ben Franklin Technology Partners. Drinks courtesy of Solid Product Design.**

​​​**Agenda**

* ​​​6:00-6:30pm: Arrive, mingle, grab some food and a beverage
* ​​​6:30-6:45pm: Opening remarks
* ​​​6:45-7:30pm: Guest Speakers + Q&A with the audience
* ​​​7:30-8:00pm: Open Mic. Anyone in attendance can give a demo or short presentation on their hardware product, startup, or project.
* ​​​8:00-8:30pm: Mix, mingle, and close out the night.

​​​
**Thank you to our partners:**

**​[Ben Franklin Technology Partners](https://benfranklin.org/)**, our food sponsor for the evening. BFTP creates and retains highly paid, sustainable jobs by investing in and linking early-stage technology firms and established manufacturers with experts, universities, follow-on funding, and other resources to help them prosper through innovation.

**[Solid Product Design](https://solidpd.com/)** is a Philadelphia-based hardware product design and development firm. We help companies ship products faster by combining deep research, first principles thinking, and a user-centered development approach. Our experienced team anticipates challenges early, helping clients identify and solve problems before they arise - so ideas move smoothly from concept to commercialization.

**​[informal](https://informal.cc/?utm_source=luma)** is a freelance collective for the most talented independent professionals in hardware and hardtech. Whether you're looking for a single contractor, a full-time employee, or an entire team of professionals to work on everything from product development to go-to-market strategy, the informal collective has the perfect collection of people for the job. With expertise in diverse projects ranging from consumer electronics to automotive, green tech, and medical devices, informal members work with clients at every stage to design, manufacture, and ship top-quality physical products, built to succeed.

name: ProductTank Philly Summer Kick-Off: Product, Pretzels, & a Breather
description:
Product work can be a grind: roadmaps, priorities, stakeholders, discovery, launches, metrics, and the occasional “quick sync” that is somehow never quick.

So we’re kicking off summer with something simpler: a relaxed evening for Philly product people to step away from the backlog, grab a drink or snack, and connect with others who understand the beautiful chaos of building products.

Join **ProductTank Philadelphia** at **Frankford Hall** on **June 3 from 6–8 PM** for a casual summer meetup with product managers, product leaders, designers, founders, builders, and product-curious folks across the Philly tech community.

No panel. No slides. No homework.
Just good conversation, fresh air, summer vibes, and a chance to recharge with people who also spend their days turning ambiguity into action.

Come as you are. Bring a product friend. Backlogs can wait.

name: PLUG Central - Jitsi Meet: 7-9PM; Speaker from 7:30-8:30PM
description:
Please join us for PLUG Central. During the pandemic we meet on-line at Jitsi Meet, beginning at 7pm. RSVPs are optional. Anyone interested in Linux and Free and Open Source software is welcome to attend. We welcome all levels of expertise, whether you're just curious, a hobbyist, or a professional.

Jitsi Meet agenda:

Q&A with emcee CJ Fearnley from 7-7:30PM. Bring your Linux (and related) questions for a group exploration.

The main presentation from 7:30-8:30PM: TBD.

Q&A continued from 8:30-9PM.

After the pandemic dissipates, we plan to again meet in Lecture Hall A in Griffith Hall at USP in Philadelphia. We plan to revert to the original speaker slot from 8-9PM with Q&A at the beginning to better support travel constraints.

After the presentation some of us adjourn to Clarkville (http://www.clarkvillephilly.com/) a nearby pizza and craft beer restaurant.

University of the Sciences in Philadelphia (USP)
Griffith Hall (Room "A" or "C", look for the signs)
600 South 43rd Street
Philadelphia, PA 19104-4495

USP is located in University City. Driving directions are available at http://www.phillylinux.org/locations/usp.html, or http://www.usip.edu/directions, both of which have an aerial view of the campus buildings. USP is also easily accessible by public transportation.

Often, the door to Griffith Hall is locked at 7PM. If you find the door locked, please contact the security guard (usually in the booth in the parking lot) and tell them that the monthly PLUG (Philadelphia Linux User Group) meeting in Griffith Hall is tonight. If there is no one in the booth you can call UCS's public safety number at 215.895.1117 and they will radio personnel to unlock the door. Our sponsor is Zhiwei Liu in the Chemistry department. Security knows this and will almost always open the door for you and have it unlocked so other participants can enter without issue.

name: The AGI Transition: How AI Affects Work and Life | Virtual Workshop Series
description:
**A free, public workshop—no technical background needed**
​Artificial intelligence is no longer just getting better at narrow tasks—it’s beginning to look *general*. Many researchers believe we’re entering the early stages of the **AGI transition**: a period where AI systems start to match or exceed human abilities across a wide range of domains.
​This workshop explores what AGI actually means, how to think about it intuitively (without hype or jargon), and what this transition could mean for your job, your relationships, and your day-to-day life—much sooner than most people expect.
​Together, we’ll explore questions like:
​**What is AGI?**
​How do experts define artificial general intelligence, and how is it different from today’s AI tools?
​**Work & Jobs:**
​Which kinds of jobs are already being automated or transformed—and which ones experts believe are most likely to remain resilient?
​**Human Connection:**
​How is AI starting to replace or supplement roles once filled by coworkers, friends, tutors, therapists, and companions?
​**What you’ll get:**

* ​A clear, beginner-friendly explanation of AGI and why it matters
* ​Real-world examples of how AI is already reshaping work and social life
* ​Live demos that highlight both impressive capabilities and emerging risks
* ​Interactive discussion and exercises to test your own intuitions about the future

​Come ready to grapple with one of the biggest transitions humanity may ever face—a shift that could redefine not just how we work, but how we relate to each other, find meaning, and navigate a world increasingly shaped by intelligent machines.
​*This is the second session in a six-part virtual series. Each workshop stands on its own and is free to attend. Future sessions: 6/9 Superintelligent AI, 6/11 Intro to AI Evals, 6/16 Data and Privacy in the Age of AI, 6/18 AI Policy and Governance Overview*

name: Superintelligent AI and Loss of Control | Virtual Workshop Series
description:
**A free, public workshop—no technical background needed**
​What would it actually mean for machines to become smarter than humans—not just at chess or writing, but at *everything*? For decades, scientists and researchers have debated the idea of Superintelligent AI: systems that could outperform humans across nearly all cognitive tasks. What once sounded like distant science fiction is now increasingly discussed as a real possibility within our lifetimes.
​This workshop is a guided, interactive introduction to the idea of superintelligence and the problem of loss of control—what happens if we create systems that are more capable than we are, but whose goals don’t perfectly align with human values.
​**Over the course of the workshop, we’ll explore:**

* ​**Superintelligence**: What it means for an AI system to surpass human intelligence across domains, and how this differs from today’s AI.
* ​**Power and control**: Why advanced AI could wield enormous economic, political, and strategic influence—and why “just turning it off” may not be realistic.
* ​**Misalignment risks**: How systems pursuing the *wrong* objectives could cause harm even without malicious intent.
* ​**Societal choices**: How decisions made today—by researchers, companies, and governments—may shape whether AI becomes a tool we control or a force we struggle to contain.

​**What you’ll get:**

* ​A clear, non-technical introduction to core ideas from AI safety and existential risk research.
* ​Live demonstrations of current AI capabilities, to ground abstract concerns in real systems.
* ​Small-group discussions and thought experiments that connect AI risks to everyday life, institutions, and incentives.
* ​A better mental model for evaluating headlines, hype, and claims about AI progress.

​**Format & vibe:**
​This is not a lecture it is an interactive workshop designed to help people think clearly about a topic that is increasingly important and moving very quickly.
​Whether you’re new to AI, casually interested, or deeply unsure what to believe, you’ll leave with a clearer picture of *why* many researchers take superintelligence seriously—and *what’s actually at stake* if we get it wrong.
​*This is the third session in a six-part virtual series. Each workshop stands on its own and is free to attend. Future sessions: 6/11 Intro to AI Evals, 6/16 Data and Privacy in the Age of AI, 6/18 AI Policy and Governance Overview*

name: The Future of Product Management & UX/UI Design in an AI-First Era
description:
[Register here to receive your access link. ](https://ctrly.io/Ypeyr )

AI is rapidly reshaping how modern products are *designed*, *built*, and *optimized*.

This conversation will examine the practical tools, workflows, and strategies helping teams build faster, smarter, and more user-centered products.

Join product, UX, and innovation leaders as they discuss how organizations are embedding AI across the product lifecycle, improving collaboration between product, design, and engineering teams, and navigating the operational challenges that come with AI adoption.

**What You'll Takeaway**

* How AI is transforming the end‑to‑end product workflow—from discovery to delivery
* Practical ways to apply AI across product management, UX, and decision‑making
* How to evaluate and integrate emerging AI tools effectively
* Strategies for navigating adoption challenges, governance, and organizational readiness
* How product, design, and engineering teams can collaborate in AI‑first environments

name: PLUG Central - Jitsi Meet: 7-9PM; Speaker from 7:30-8:30PM
description:
Please join us for PLUG Central. During the pandemic we meet on-line at Jitsi Meet, beginning at 7pm. RSVPs are optional. Anyone interested in Linux and Free and Open Source software is welcome to attend. We welcome all levels of expertise, whether you're just curious, a hobbyist, or a professional.

Jitsi Meet agenda:

Q&A with emcee CJ Fearnley from 7-7:30PM. Bring your Linux (and related) questions for a group exploration.

The main presentation from 7:30-8:30PM: TBD.

Q&A continued from 8:30-9PM.

After the pandemic dissipates, we plan to again meet in Lecture Hall A in Griffith Hall at USP in Philadelphia. We plan to revert to the original speaker slot from 8-9PM with Q&A at the beginning to better support travel constraints.

After the presentation some of us adjourn to Clarkville (http://www.clarkvillephilly.com/) a nearby pizza and craft beer restaurant.

University of the Sciences in Philadelphia (USP)
Griffith Hall (Room "A" or "C", look for the signs)
600 South 43rd Street
Philadelphia, PA 19104-4495

USP is located in University City. Driving directions are available at http://www.phillylinux.org/locations/usp.html, or http://www.usip.edu/directions, both of which have an aerial view of the campus buildings. USP is also easily accessible by public transportation.

Often, the door to Griffith Hall is locked at 7PM. If you find the door locked, please contact the security guard (usually in the booth in the parking lot) and tell them that the monthly PLUG (Philadelphia Linux User Group) meeting in Griffith Hall is tonight. If there is no one in the booth you can call UCS's public safety number at 215.895.1117 and they will radio personnel to unlock the door. Our sponsor is Zhiwei Liu in the Chemistry department. Security knows this and will almost always open the door for you and have it unlocked so other participants can enter without issue.
